about.title: About the Group
about.description: Software Engineering: Research, Development, and Education (SERDE) is a research group created in 2016 at the Federal University of Amapá (UNIFAP). The group was born from the recognition that academic training in Computer Science often does not sufficiently cover the practice of Software Engineering, leading professionals to learn their main activities only in the job market. This gap contributes to the lack of qualified specialists and to the difficulty of the industry in finding professionals prepared to work in software development processes. SERDE aims to produce scientific knowledge in the area of Software Engineering, while developing methodologies, tools, and practices that bridge theory and practice, fostering both education and professional application.
about.description2: The group also seeks to strengthen academic training by promoting experiences that connect students to real-world challenges, as well as stimulating technological innovation as a driver of regional and social development. One of its distinguishing features is having emerged rooted in the Amazonian reality, with the conviction that software quality and digital transformation can be instruments for sustainable development. More than creating technological solutions, SERDE is dedicated to evaluating the effective impact of their use, ensuring that innovation produces concrete results for society and contributes to building a solid ecosystem of knowledge and innovation in the region.
about.projects_description: Projects developed by the group, ongoing or already completed
about.publications_description: Scientific production of the group, including articles, books, and academic papers
about.guidance_description: Academic works completed under the guidance of the group's professors.
about.members_description: Professors and students who are part of the research group

innovation.title: Innovation & Entrepreneurship
innovation.intro: The SERDE group works in an integrated manner with the innovation ecosystem, encouraging the transformation of ideas into technological solutions with real impact.
innovation.intro2: Through student training and applied project development, several initiatives have gained recognition in national funding programs.
